profile.name: Sanju Chowdhury
profile.title: Software developer turned MBA candidate — applying 2 years of engineering execution to product strategy, GTM, and 0→1 building.
profile.focus: Building enterprise product strategy and data-driven GTM execution.
profile.transitionMoment: While building a dashboard module at AssetCues, I noticed customers bypassing our automated alerts to query raw data directly. Pushing for customizable reports taught me that bridging technical execution with customer empathy is where real value is created.
profile.summary: MBA candidate in Technology Management (IPL Bengaluru) with 2 years of software engineering experience. Focused on bridging engineering execution with product strategy, GTM validation, and 0→1 building.

experience.description: Enterprise software development agency, coordinating feature delivery and mapping backend performance to core user experience.
bulletPoints: Collaborated with product teams to scope feature delivery for 10+ core modules, identifying and reducing backend integration bottlenecks to trim implementation timelines by 15%.
Refactored user-facing search queries because data logs showed a 40% drop-off rate on slower page loads, which recovered user session length by 12%.

experience.description: Cross-platform mobile travel startup developing consumer reservation and account management applications.
bulletPoints: Developed cross-platform mobile booking flows using Flutter so that users could complete reservations in under three taps, increasing registration conversions by 8%.
Implemented responsive API routes to unify travel databases, eliminating cross-platform latency to deliver booking confirmations instantly.

experience.description: B2B SaaS enterprise asset tracking platform, designing customer dashboards and backend data optimizations.
bulletPoints: Designed real-time asset monitoring dashboards to reduce manual data tracking efforts by 40% for enterprise users managing 1,000+ assets.
Restructured database indexes to optimize API response times from 3.5s to under 800ms, ensuring stable analytics views for high-concurrency client sessions.

experience.company: Retail Scan Management Services Pvt. Ltd.
experience.description: Retail market intelligence and store audit agency translating ground-level feedback into growth strategies.
bulletPoints: Conducted competitive research and direct client outreach to define target segments, feeding local market feedback into product teams to shape new B2B feature requirements.
Translated field user feedback into structured feature requests so that product teams could improve offline-data-sync performance for field executives.

projects.title: Karigold
projects.description: A pioneering D2C gold jewelry digital platform that directly maps verified certified karigars to modern retail customers.
projects.context: India's ₹5.5L Cr gold jewelry market suffers from trust deficits and markups. Built this platform to validate if transparent karigar-to-consumer links could reshape buying trust.
projects.problem: Traditional retailers charge high making fees (up to 25%) while artisans receive less than 5%. Customers lack verified traceability of gold purity (HUID).
projects.process: Conducted 100+ customer interviews and analyzed 6 leading jewelry brands to map purchasing criteria and artisan earnings distributions.
projects.solutionTradeoffs: Defined product strategy for a direct-to-consumer marketplace with HUID traceability, custom jewelry design, and fair pay for verified karigars. Decided not to build a custom payment gateway initially, opting to integrate off-the-shelf UPI to prioritize launch speed.
projects.expectedOutcome: Hypothesized to lower consumer premiums by 15% while doubling artisan earnings compared to traditional retail channels.
projects.whatToTestNext: Deploy a lightweight landing page with high-fidelity custom design examples to measure email signup rates and deposit commitments.

projects.title: Nexus
projects.tagline: Product Lead | B2B SaaS | Skillathon – Value Proposition Design
projects.description: A robust B2B operations and event management platform targeting the complex MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) sector.
projects.context: Event coordination in the ₹10k Cr MICE industry is highly fragmented, relying on ad-hoc WhatsApp communication and manual tracking sheets.
projects.problem: Event managers lose up to 10% of their operational budget on unverified vendor SLA delays and untracked changes.
projects.process: Created user journey maps for event managers, mapped vendor touchpoints, and conducted stakeholder feedback loops with 10 team organizers.
projects.solutionTradeoffs: Proposed a unified SaaS platform with central SLA tracking, dynamic vendor dashboards, and instant budget alerts. Deferred building real-time messaging, prioritizing vendor dashboard access instead. Awarded 1st place of 10 teams at IPL Skillathon.
projects.expectedOutcome: Estimated to save up to 12 hours of manual sync time per event week and reduce budget leakages by 5%.
projects.whatToTestNext: Run a closed beta with 3 local event organizing agencies using an interactive Figma prototype to validate user path completion rates.

projects.title: Parkhive
projects.description: An intelligent, sensor-driven parking management and slot booking system designed to reduce city traffic congestion.
projects.context: Urban congestion is exacerbated by drivers looking for parking spaces. Studied smart parking SaaS to find optimal slot utility models.
projects.problem: Multi-story garage parking spaces remain underutilized (average 65% capacity) while nearby street congestion grows because drivers lack real-time space visibility.
projects.process: Simulated parking slot utilization data and benchmarked existing reservation paths on leading smart city applications.
projects.solutionTradeoffs: Designed dynamic slot-allocation logic that automatically routes incoming vehicles to underutilized garage levels. Excluded license-plate-recognition setup for v1, opting for standard QR codes to minimize deployment overhead.
projects.expectedOutcome: Simulations modeled a 22% improvement in space utility rates and a 15% drop in average search time.
projects.whatToTestNext: Integrate real-time occupancy sensor feedback APIs and test dynamic pricing curves based on hourly demand changes.

projects.title: My Fitness Pal
projects.description: A custom health analytics client cloning and enhancing the core fitness tracking, activity log, and calorie intake visualization flows.
projects.context: Fitness tracking apps often suffer from high churn due to complex logging steps. Rebuilt this interface to experiment with high-speed macro input loops.
projects.problem: Manual calorie logging takes an average of 45 seconds per meal, creating immediate churn points for busy users.
projects.process: Conducted heuristic evaluation of calorie search bars, mapped user paths, and analyzed chart responsiveness.
projects.solutionTradeoffs: Engineered a rapid macro logging dashboard with interactive charts translating dietary logs into macro breakdowns in under 15 seconds. Chose to skip barcode scanning in the initial client mockup to focus on the core user navigation speed.
projects.expectedOutcome: Hypothesized that reducing time-to-log by 60% would increase day-14 logging retention by 10%.
projects.whatToTestNext: Deploy an AI-enabled voice-to-text logging widget to measure input error rates and user preferences.

projects.title: Anthill
projects.tagline: Product Manager | Team Feedback & Project Ops
projects.description: A collaborative workspace organizing project feedback loops, user story mapping, and agile sprint delivery status.
projects.context: Agile teams face project drift due to misaligned priorities between customer support requests and engineering backlogs.
projects.problem: Critical customer feedback takes weeks to get filtered into the developer backlog, leading to churn and delayed updates.
projects.process: Audited Jira backlog prioritization flows and mapped timeline bottlenecks between support tickets and product specifications.
projects.solutionTradeoffs: Designed centralized backlog mapping boards prioritizing items automatically via custom RICE framework weights. Excluded automatic Slack triggers, focusing instead on dashboard prioritization metrics.
projects.expectedOutcome: Targeted a 30% reduction in lead time for shipping high-priority customer feature requests.
projects.whatToTestNext: Integrate customer support ticketing APIs directly to measure RICE calculation speed and stakeholder review ratings.

projects.title: Solar Grid
projects.description: An interactive, visual grid manager for analyzing solar panel layout density, solar capture rates, and energy generation output.
projects.context: Solar installers spend hours drawing placement grids and calculating shadows manually during sales pitches.
projects.problem: Manual placement grids result in a 10% discrepancy in solar potential, causing post-installation complaints and cost overruns.
projects.process: Modeled shadow zone vectors and sunlight angle variations using HTML5 canvas and geometric math equations.
projects.solutionTradeoffs: Engineered a drag-and-drop solar placement canvas that updates generation output and shadow zones instantly. Decided to simulate sunlight angles geometrically rather than fetching raw weather API curves, saving 3 weeks of API setup.
projects.expectedOutcome: Estimated to cut sales-pitch proposal preparation times from 3 hours to 10 minutes.
projects.whatToTestNext: Integrate geographical mapping APIs to pull local historical weather patterns and refine solar calculations.

projects.title: Anthropic
projects.description: Enterprise AI strategy study evaluating B2B GTM advantages, multi-cloud distribution, and revenue monetization models.
projects.context: While competitors chased consumer scale, Anthropic bet that the enterprise buyer — slower, pickier, but stickier — was the better business to build.
projects.problem: Frontier AI model intelligence is commoditizing rapidly, turning consumer chatbots into highly elastic products with high churn rates.
projects.process: Mapped enterprise MAU monetization metrics, evaluated compute delivery costs, and structured multi-cloud serving stack parities.
projects.solutionTradeoffs: Proposed expanding safety-centric API integrations and serving model parity across vertex AI, Bedrock, and Azure. Avoided custom hardware investment to focus strictly on serving stack compatibility.
projects.expectedOutcome: Model showcases an 8x revenue advantage per user ($192/MAU vs OpenAI's $23/MAU) driven by enterprise seat-license pricing.
projects.whatToTestNext: A/B test developer conversion rates for safety APIs versus performance-driven API endpoints.

projects.title: CRED
projects.description: Trust-based monetization and premium audience-access model teardown of India's top 1% fintech credit club.
projects.context: A free app for India's top 1% of credit card holders sounds like a vanity project — until you notice Meta just paid $900M to be part of it.
projects.problem: Excluding 99% of India's population limits traditional growth loops, making monetization of the restricted member base a high-friction challenge.
projects.process: Audited transaction data paths, mapped premium advertising conversion loops, and evaluated post-funding valuation trajectories.
projects.solutionTradeoffs: Monetized the club through high-value partner offers and credit facilities. Decided to skip general display ads to maintain clean, high-end UX integrity.
projects.expectedOutcome: Proved that premium membership curation triggers extremely high ad conversion and merchant ROI, justifying Meta's $4.5B valuation investment.
projects.whatToTestNext: Validate behavioral scoring models against direct credit card transaction defaults to evaluate risk prediction.

projects.title: Blinkit
projects.description: Quick commerce operational model and unit economics study tracking the transition from marketplace to first-party inventory profitability.
projects.context: Blinkit turned its first adjusted EBITDA profit in Q4 FY26 — on a margin of 0.3% of order value. The real story isn't the win, it's how thin the win still is.
projects.problem: Quick commerce operates on extremely low margins, where tier-2 city expansion scales operational overhead without guaranteed order density.
projects.process: Analyzed adjusted EBITDA margins, wastage metrics, dark store density configurations, and brand media revenue loops.
projects.solutionTradeoffs: Focused GTM expansion strictly on store density and first-party inventory control. Decided to defer tier-2 aggressive store expansion until demand forecasting models mature.
projects.expectedOutcome: Demonstrated 0.3% EBITDA swing into profitability, with retail ads acting as the key margin driver to bridge the 5% long-term target gap.
projects.whatToTestNext: A/B test different service fee thresholds across low-density vs high-density store circles to map order drop-off patterns.

frameworks.name: RICE Prioritization
frameworks.description: A framework to score feature backlogs by comparing Reach, Impact, Confidence, and Effort.
frameworks.example: Applied at Anthill to prioritize a customer feedback backlog: deferred a high-effort integration (Effort: 5) to expedite a high-reach, high-confidence dashboard filter (Reach: 80% active users, Confidence: 90%), delivering a 30% reduction in customer response times.

frameworks.name: Jobs-to-be-Done (JTBD)
frameworks.description: A methodology to understand the deep functional and emotional situations driving customers to buy or use a product.
frameworks.example: Used in the Karigold study: reframed the customer job from 'buying gold' to 'securing authentic, certified jewelry with direct traceability and transparent artisan pricing,' resulting in a trust-centric D2C model.

frameworks.name: North Star Metric
frameworks.description: The key measure of product success that captures the core value delivered to customers.
frameworks.example: Defined 'Day-7 Active Workgroup Collaborations' as the North Star for the Notion Case Study, aligning product onboarding experiments specifically to drive user-to-user document sharing within 3 minutes of signup.
